{"id":"smallhold-8k8","title":"DB repository pattern: typed queries, no raw SQL in handlers","description":"Establish the repository pattern for all DB access. No handler should contain raw SQL strings — all queries live in db/ module as functions that take typed parameters and return typed structs. Use sqlx::query_as!() and sqlx::query!() with compile-time checking. Create repositories for: accounts, posts, remote_accounts, follows, notifications (initial set).

Agent workflow: Spawn a subagent to audit all query patterns and ensure query_as!() compile-time checking is in use (not query() which skips type checking).

Test discipline:
- Every repository function has at least one test using a real in-memory SQLite database (not mocked)
- Tests insert known rows, call repository function, verify returned struct fields exactly
- Test error cases: calling get_account(999) when ID doesn't exist returns Err(NotFound), not panic

Defensive programming:
- All IDs in repository functions are typed (Id newtype), not raw i64 — prevents accidental swap of account_id for post_id
- Pagination parameters (limit, before_id) are validated: limit in [1, 40], before_id is non-negative","acceptance_criteria":"cargo build with SQLX_OFFLINE=false succeeds (queries are checked at compile time); no raw SQL strings in non-db modules; all repository functions have integration tests with real SQLite","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:27:02Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:27:02Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-cq9","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:07Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
